plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to eliminate risk.
Sell existing semiconductor (SMH) and income (QQQI) holdings.
Increase allocation to broad index (SPY) for stability.
Add new positions in Amazon (AMZN) for quality tech exposure.
Add healthcare (XLV) for sector diversification and defense.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation with reduced leverage.
conservative.read: This plan aims for low single-digit monthly returns with significantly reduced risk. Expect smaller potential gains and a lower chance of large losses.

plan: Reduce exposure to leveraged ETF (SOXL) and trim semiconductors (SMH).
Trim income ETF (QQQI) to free up capital.
Increase allocation to broad index (SPY) for stability.
Add Amazon (AMZN) for quality tech exposure.
Initiate a new position in ZS for growth potential.
This plan balances risk reduction with growth opportunities.
balanced.read: This plan targets modest monthly returns while actively managing risk. It seeks to reduce concentration and leverage while adding quality names.

plan: Increase allocation to leveraged ETF (SOXL) and semiconductors (SMH).
Exit income ETF (QQQI) to reallocate capital.
Reduce broad index (SPY) allocation to concentrate risk.
Add AMZU and ZS, top-rated tech names from scanner.
Add ERX, a high-scoring energy play, for diversification.
This plan aggressively pursues growth using leverage and concentration.
aggressive.read: This plan aims for higher monthly returns but carries significantly elevated risk, including larger potential drawdowns. Leveraged ETF decay is a key risk.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) and high-beta growth (CRWD, SMH).
Significantly increase allocation to income ETF (QQQI).
Increase broad index (SPY) for stability and income potential.
Add healthcare (XLV) for defensive sector exposure.
This plan prioritizes steady income generation over growth.
Focus is on capital preservation and consistent distributions.
income.read: This plan focuses on generating steady monthly income with minimal risk. Expect low growth potential and limited downside in most market conditions.
